[[Category:藤沢市の神社]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine (諏訪神社 (藤沢市片瀬))}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages]][[ja:諏訪神社 (藤沢市片瀬)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages]][[Category:needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:諏訪神社 (藤沢市片瀬)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages]][[Category:needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:諏訪神社 (藤沢市片瀬)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Unsorted_completion_categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[sn:諏訪神社 (藤沢市片瀬)]][[Category:Unsorted_completion_categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] {{Shrine |名称 = {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社}} |image = {{ill|File:Suwa-jinja 001.jpg|ja|Yasaka Shrine Main Hall|lt=Yasaka Shrine Main Hall|lt_ja=八坂神社 本殿}}
Shimosha Torii |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} |location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}{{ill|Fujisawa City|ja|藤沢市}}{{ill|Katase (Fujisawa City)|ja|片瀬 (藤沢市)|lt=Katase|lt_ja=片瀬}} 2-chōme 21-16 (Shimosha) |位置 = Shimosha: {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|19|2.6|N|139|29|11.5|E|display=inline,title}}
Kamisha: {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|19|3.7|N|139|29|19.6|E|display=inline}} |deity = {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=建御名方命}}、{{ill|Yasakatotome-no-Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|lt=Yasakatotome-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=八坂刀売命}} |神体 = |type = Former village shrine (旧{{ill|Murasya|ja|村社}}) |創建 = In the 7th year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} ({{ill|723 AD|ja|723年}}) |本殿 = |別名 = Also known as Katase Suwa Shrine (片瀬諏訪神社) |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|August 27|ja|8月27日}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kanagawa|label=Suwa Shrine|float=center|width=210|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|35|19|2.6|N|139|29|11.5|E}}}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社, すわじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Fujisawa City|ja|藤沢市}}, Katase. It is also known as Katase Suwa Shrine (片瀬諏訪神社). The shrine is divided into two sections: the western Shimosha (lower shrine) and the eastern Kamisha (upper shrine). == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}} (Takeminakata-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Yasakatotome-no-Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命}} (Yasakatotome-no-Mikoto) == History == The shrine was founded in the 7th year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} ({{ill|723 AD|ja|723年}}). It enshrines the divine spirit of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, making it the oldest among those that received a portion of the spirit (御分霊) sent to other regions. The Shimosha was relocated from Miyabata in 812 AD (the 3rd year of {{ill|Kōnin|ja|弘仁}}), to a lakeside location with whale bones, while the Kamisha was relocated in 826 AD (the 3rd year of {{ill|Tenchō|ja|天長}}) from Suwa Gayatani to the mountainside of Nami-ai. In 1873 (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a village shrine. The shrine building of the Shimosha was rebuilt in 1941 (Showa 16), along with a new administrative office. Additionally, the one-bay shrine ({{ill|Ryū-zukuri|ja|流造}}) at the Kamisha was completed as part of the 60th Anniversary Celebration of the reign of the {{ill|Showa Emperor|ja|昭和天皇}}.[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=535&cd=1206019&scd=&npg=0 Suwa Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) In Reiwa 5 (2023), the shrine celebrated 1300 years since its establishment, with a 1300th Anniversary Celebration held on October 22. == Annual Festivals == * New Year's Festival (元旦祭): January 1 * Tanabata Festival: Mid-June to July 7 * Summer Grand Festival: ** August 23: Banner Raising, Procession of the Sacred Object, Temporary Palace Wearing Ritual, and commencement announcement for the Upper Suwa Shrine Festival ** August 26: Evening Palace Ceremony ** August 27: Beach Descent Ceremony, Festival (unspecified), and Divine Blessing Festival ** August 28: Return Procession and conclusion announcement for the Upper Suwa Shrine Festival * Niiname-sai (新嘗祭): Late November (on Sunday) * Great Purification Ritual (大祓): December 31 == Local Information == ; Address: * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Fujisawa City|ja|藤沢市}}, Katase 2-chōme, 21-16 ; Transportation: * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kintetsu South Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}{{ill|Fujisawa Station|ja|藤沢駅}}; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|和書|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣}}|date=August 1932}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=535&cd=1206019&scd=&npg=0 {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社}}] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) *[http://katasesuwajinja.com/ Katase Suwa Shrine] (Official) *[http://www.suwayamyoujin.jp/ {{ill|Suwa Shrine Official Homepage|ja|諏訪神社 公式ホームページ}}) {{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すわしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Fujisawa City]] [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages, need dummy edit imported for attribution, if they are redirects the redirects need to be reviewed, adding jawiki link which must be reviewed to see if the page is already present here. Later on the categories should be renamed too.]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (Fujisawa City Katase)]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages]][[Category:needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]] [[Category:Unsorted_completion_categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Qq-1月4日 (旧暦)-qq]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (Fujisawa City Katase)]]

approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|和書|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣}}|date=August 1932}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=535&cd=1206019&scd=&npg=0 {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社}}] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) *[http://katasesuwajinja.com/ Katase Suwa Shrine] (Official) *[http://www.suwayamyoujin.jp/ {{ill|Suwa Shrine Official Homepage|ja|諏訪神社 公式ホームページ}}) {{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すわしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Fujisawa City]] [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages, need dummy edit imported for attribution, if they are redirects the redirects need to be reviewed, adding jawiki link which must be reviewed to see if the page is already present here. revision.text:
[[Category:藤沢市の神社]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine (諏訪神社 (藤沢市片瀬))}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages]][[ja:諏訪神社 (藤沢市片瀬)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages]][[Category:needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:諏訪神社 (藤沢市片瀬)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages]][[Category:needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:諏訪神社 (藤沢市片瀬)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[sn:諏訪神社 (藤沢市片瀬)]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] {{Shrine |名称 = {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社}} |image = {{ill|File:Suwa-jinja 001.jpg|ja|Yasaka Shrine Main Hall|lt=Yasaka Shrine Main Hall|lt_ja=八坂神社 本殿}}
Shimosha Torii |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} |location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}{{ill|Fujisawa City|ja|藤沢市}}{{ill|Katase (Fujisawa City)|ja|片瀬 (藤沢市)|lt=Katase|lt_ja=片瀬}} 2-chōme 21-16 (Shimosha) |位置 = Shimosha: {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|19|2.6|N|139|29|11.5|E|display=inline,title}}
Kamisha: {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|19|3.7|N|139|29|19.6|E|display=inline}} |deity = {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=建御名方命}}、{{ill|Yasakatotome-no-Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|lt=Yasakatotome-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=八坂刀売命}} |神体 = |type = Former village shrine (旧{{ill|Murasya|ja|村社}}) |創建 = In the 7th year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} ({{ill|723 AD|ja|723年}}) |本殿 = |別名 = Also known as Katase Suwa Shrine (片瀬諏訪神社) |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|August 27|ja|8月27日}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kanagawa|label=Suwa Shrine|float=center|width=210|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|35|19|2.6|N|139|29|11.5|E}}}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社, すわじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Fujisawa City|ja|藤沢市}}, Katase. It is also known as Katase Suwa Shrine (片瀬諏訪神社). The shrine is divided into two sections: the western Shimosha (lower shrine) and the eastern Kamisha (upper shrine). == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}} (Takeminakata-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Yasakatotome-no-Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命}} (Yasakatotome-no-Mikoto) == History == The shrine was founded in the 7th year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} ({{ill|723 AD|ja|723年}}). It enshrines the divine spirit of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, making it the oldest among those that received a portion of the spirit (御分霊) sent to other regions. The Shimosha was relocated from Miyabata in 812 AD (the 3rd year of {{ill|Kōnin|ja|弘仁}}), to a lakeside location with whale bones, while the Kamisha was relocated in 826 AD (the 3rd year of {{ill|Tenchō|ja|天長}}) from Suwa Gayatani to the mountainside of Nami-ai. In 1873 (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a village shrine. The shrine building of the Shimosha was rebuilt in 1941 (Showa 16), along with a new administrative office. Additionally, the one-bay shrine ({{ill|Ryū-zukuri|ja|流造}}) at the Kamisha was completed as part of the 60th Anniversary Celebration of the reign of the {{ill|Showa Emperor|ja|昭和天皇}}.[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=535&cd=1206019&scd=&npg=0 Suwa Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) In Reiwa 5 (2023), the shrine celebrated 1300 years since its establishment, with a 1300th Anniversary Celebration held on October 22. == Annual Festivals == * New Year's Festival (元旦祭): January 1 * Tanabata Festival: Mid-June to July 7 * Summer Grand Festival: ** August 23: Banner Raising, Procession of the Sacred Object, Temporary Palace Wearing Ritual, and commencement announcement for the Upper Suwa Shrine Festival ** August 26: Evening Palace Ceremony ** August 27: Beach Descent Ceremony, Festival (unspecified), and Divine Blessing Festival ** August 28: Return Procession and conclusion announcement for the Upper Suwa Shrine Festival * Niiname-sai (新嘗祭): Late November (on Sunday) * Great Purification Ritual (大祓): December 31 == Local Information == ; Address: * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Fujisawa City|ja|藤沢市}}, Katase 2-chōme, 21-16 ; Transportation: * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kintetsu South Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}{{ill|Fujisawa Station|ja|藤沢駅}}; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|和書|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣}}|date=August 1932}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=535&cd=1206019&scd=&npg=0 {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社}}] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) *[http://katasesuwajinja.com/ Katase Suwa Shrine] (Official) *[http://www.suwayamyoujin.jp/ {{ill|Suwa Shrine Official Homepage|ja|諏訪神社 公式ホームページ}}) {{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すわしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Fujisawa City]] [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages, need dummy edit imported for attribution, if they are redirects the redirects need to be reviewed, adding jawiki link which must be reviewed to see if the page is already present here. Later on the categories should be renamed too.]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (Fujisawa City Katase)]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages]][[Category:needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Qq-1月4日 (旧暦)-qq]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (Fujisawa City Katase)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine (Katase, Fujisawa City)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine (Katase, Fujisawa City)}}

approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|和書|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣}}|date=August 1932}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=535&cd=1206019&scd=&npg=0 {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社}}] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) *[http://katasesuwajinja.com/ Katase Suwa Shrine] (Official) *[http://www.suwayamyoujin.jp/ {{ill|Suwa Shrine Official Homepage|ja|諏訪神社 公式ホームページ}}) {{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すわしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Fujisawa City]] [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages, need dummy edit imported for attribution, if they are redirects the redirects need to be reviewed, adding jawiki link which must be reviewed to see if the page is already present here. [[Category:藤沢市の神社]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine (諏訪神社 (藤沢市片瀬))}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages]][[ja:諏訪神社 (藤沢市片瀬)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages]][[Category:needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:諏訪神社 (藤沢市片瀬)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages]][[Category:needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:諏訪神社 (藤沢市片瀬)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:諏訪神社 (藤沢市片瀬)]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] {{Shrine |名称 = {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社}} |image = {{ill|File:Suwa-jinja 001.jpg|ja|Yasaka Shrine Main Hall|lt=Yasaka Shrine Main Hall|lt_ja=八坂神社 本殿}}
Shimosha Torii |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} |location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}{{ill|Fujisawa City|ja|藤沢市}}{{ill|Katase (Fujisawa City)|ja|片瀬 (藤沢市)|lt=Katase|lt_ja=片瀬}} 2-chōme 21-16 (Shimosha) |位置 = Shimosha: {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|19|2.6|N|139|29|11.5|E|display=inline,title}}
Kamisha: {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|19|3.7|N|139|29|19.6|E|display=inline}} |deity = {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=建御名方命}}、{{ill|Yasakatotome-no-Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|lt=Yasakatotome-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=八坂刀売命}} |神体 = |type = Former village shrine (旧{{ill|Murasya|ja|村社}}) |創建 = In the 7th year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} ({{ill|723 AD|ja|723年}}) |本殿 = |別名 = Also known as Katase Suwa Shrine (片瀬諏訪神社) |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|August 27|ja|8月27日}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kanagawa|label=Suwa Shrine|float=center|width=210|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|35|19|2.6|N|139|29|11.5|E}}}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社, すわじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Fujisawa City|ja|藤沢市}}, Katase. It is also known as Katase Suwa Shrine (片瀬諏訪神社). The shrine is divided into two sections: the western Shimosha (lower shrine) and the eastern Kamisha (upper shrine). == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}} (Takeminakata-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Yasakatotome-no-Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命}} (Yasakatotome-no-Mikoto) == History == The shrine was founded in the 7th year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} ({{ill|723 AD|ja|723年}}). It enshrines the divine spirit of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, making it the oldest among those that received a portion of the spirit (御分霊) sent to other regions. The Shimosha was relocated from Miyabata in 812 AD (the 3rd year of {{ill|Kōnin|ja|弘仁}}), to a lakeside location with whale bones, while the Kamisha was relocated in 826 AD (the 3rd year of {{ill|Tenchō|ja|天長}}) from Suwa Gayatani to the mountainside of Nami-ai. In 1873 (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a village shrine. The shrine building of the Shimosha was rebuilt in 1941 (Showa 16), along with a new administrative office. Additionally, the one-bay shrine ({{ill|Ryū-zukuri|ja|流造}}) at the Kamisha was completed as part of the 60th Anniversary Celebration of the reign of the {{ill|Showa Emperor|ja|昭和天皇}}.[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=535&cd=1206019&scd=&npg=0 Suwa Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) In Reiwa 5 (2023), the shrine celebrated 1300 years since its establishment, with a 1300th Anniversary Celebration held on October 22. == Annual Festivals == * New Year's Festival (元旦祭): January 1 * Tanabata Festival: Mid-June to July 7 * Summer Grand Festival: ** August 23: Banner Raising, Procession of the Sacred Object, Temporary Palace Wearing Ritual, and commencement announcement for the Upper Suwa Shrine Festival ** August 26: Evening Palace Ceremony ** August 27: Beach Descent Ceremony, Festival (unspecified), and Divine Blessing Festival ** August 28: Return Procession and conclusion announcement for the Upper Suwa Shrine Festival * Niiname-sai (新嘗祭): Late November (on Sunday) * Great Purification Ritual (大祓): December 31 == Local Information == ; Address: * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Fujisawa City|ja|藤沢市}}, Katase 2-chōme, 21-16 ; Transportation: * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kintetsu South Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}{{ill|Fujisawa Station|ja|藤沢駅}}; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|和書|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣}}|date=August 1932}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=535&cd=1206019&scd=&npg=0 {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社}}] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) *[http://katasesuwajinja.com/ Katase Suwa Shrine] (Official) *[http://www.suwayamyoujin.jp/ {{ill|Suwa Shrine Official Homepage|ja|諏訪神社 公式ホームページ}}) {{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すわしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Fujisawa City]] [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages, need dummy edit imported for attribution, if they are redirects the redirects need to be reviewed, adding jawiki link which must be reviewed to see if the page is already present here. Later on the categories should be renamed too.]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (Fujisawa City Katase)]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages]][[Category:needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (Fujisawa City Katase)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine (Katase, Fujisawa City)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine (Katase, Fujisawa City)}}

Address: * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Fujisawa City|ja|藤沢市}}, Katase 2-chōme, 21-16 ; Transportation: * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kintetsu South Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}{{ill|Fujisawa Station|ja|藤沢駅}}; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|和書|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣}}|date=August 1932}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=535&cd=1206019&scd=&npg=0 {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社}}] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) *[http://katasesuwajinja.com/ Katase Suwa Shrine] (Official) *[http://www.suwayamyoujin.jp/ {{ill|Suwa Shrine Official Homepage|ja|諏訪神社 公式ホームページ}}) {{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すわしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Fujisawa City]] [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages, need dummy edit imported for attribution, if they are redirects the redirects need to be reviewed, adding jawiki link which must be reviewed to see if the page is already present here. Later on the categories should be renamed too.]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (Fujisawa City Katase)]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages]][[Category:needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Suwa Shrine (Katase, Fujisawa City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Suwa Shrine (Katase, Fujisawa City)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (Fujisawa City Katase)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine (Katase, Fujisawa City)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine (Katase, Fujisawa City)}}

Shimosha Torii |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} |location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}{{ill|Fujisawa City|ja|藤沢市}}{{ill|Katase (Fujisawa City)|ja|片瀬 (藤沢市)|lt=Katase|lt_ja=片瀬}} 2-chōme 21-16 (Shimosha) |位置 = Shimosha: {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|19|2.6|N|139|29|11.5|E|display=inline,title}}
Kamisha: {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|19|3.7|N|139|29|19.6|E|display=inline}} |deity = {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=建御名方命}}、{{ill|Yasakatotome-no-Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|lt=Yasakatotome-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=八坂刀売命}} |神体 = |type = Former village shrine (旧{{ill|Murasya|ja|村社}}) |創建 = In the 7th year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} ({{ill|723 AD|ja|723年}}) |本殿 = |別名 = Also known as Katase Suwa Shrine (片瀬諏訪神社) |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|August 27|ja|8月27日}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kanagawa|label=Suwa Shrine|float=center|width=210|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|35|19|2.6|N|139|29|11.5|E}}}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社, すわじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Fujisawa City|ja|藤沢市}}, Katase. It is also known as Katase Suwa Shrine (片瀬諏訪神社). The shrine is divided into two sections: the western Shimosha (lower shrine) and the eastern Kamisha (upper shrine). == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}} (Takeminakata-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Yasakatotome-no-Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命}} (Yasakatotome-no-Mikoto) == History == The shrine was founded in the 7th year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} ({{ill|723 AD|ja|723年}}). It enshrines the divine spirit of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, making it the oldest among those that received a portion of the spirit (御分霊) sent to other regions. The Shimosha was relocated from Miyabata in 812 AD (the 3rd year of {{ill|Kōnin|ja|弘仁}}), to a lakeside location with whale bones, while the Kamisha was relocated in 826 AD (the 3rd year of {{ill|Tenchō|ja|天長}}) from Suwa Gayatani to the mountainside of Nami-ai. In 1873 (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a village shrine. The shrine building of the Shimosha was rebuilt in 1941 (Showa 16), along with a new administrative office. Additionally, the one-bay shrine ({{ill|Ryū-zukuri|ja|流造}}) at the Kamisha was completed as part of the 60th Anniversary Celebration of the reign of the {{ill|Showa Emperor|ja|昭和天皇}}.[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=535&cd=1206019&scd=&npg=0 Suwa Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) In Reiwa 5 (2023), the shrine celebrated 1300 years since its establishment, with a 1300th Anniversary Celebration held on October 22. == Annual Festivals == * New Year's Festival (元旦祭): January 1 * Tanabata Festival: Mid-June to July 7 * Summer Grand Festival: ** August 23: Banner Raising, Procession of the Sacred Object, Temporary Palace Wearing Ritual, and commencement announcement for the Upper Suwa Shrine Festival ** August 26: Evening Palace Ceremony ** August 27: Beach Descent Ceremony, Festival (unspecified), and Divine Blessing Festival ** August 28: Return Procession and conclusion announcement for the Upper Suwa Shrine Festival * Niiname-sai (新嘗祭): Late November (on Sunday) * Great Purification Ritual (大祓): December 31 == Local Information == ; Address: * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Fujisawa City|ja|藤沢市}}, Katase 2-chōme, 21-16 ; Transportation: * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kintetsu South Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}{{ill|Fujisawa Station|ja|藤沢駅}}; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|和書|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣}}|date=August 1932}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=535&cd=1206019&scd=&npg=0 {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社}}] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) *[http://katasesuwajinja.com/ Katase Suwa Shrine] (Official) *[http://www.suwayamyoujin.jp/ {{ill|Suwa Shrine Official Homepage|ja|諏訪神社 公式ホームページ}}) {{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すわしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Fujisawa City]] [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages, need dummy edit imported for attribution, if they are redirects the redirects need to be reviewed, adding jawiki link which must be reviewed to see if the page is already present here. Later on the categories should be renamed too.]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (Fujisawa City Katase)]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages]][[Category:needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Suwa Shrine (Katase, Fujisawa City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Suwa Shrine (Katase, Fujisawa City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Suwa Shrine (Katase, Fujisawa City)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (Fujisawa City Katase)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine (Katase, Fujisawa City)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine (Katase, Fujisawa City)}}

Additionally, the one-bay shrine ({{ill|Ryū-zukuri|ja|流造}}) at the Kamisha was completed as part of the 60th Anniversary Celebration of the reign of the {{ill|Showa Emperor|ja|昭和天皇}}.[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=535&cd=1206019&scd=&npg=0 Suwa Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) In Reiwa 5 (2023), the shrine celebrated 1300 years since its establishment, with a 1300th Anniversary Celebration held on October 22. == Annual Festivals == * New Year's Festival (元旦祭): January 1 * Tanabata Festival: Mid-June to July 7 * Summer Grand Festival: ** August 23: Banner Raising, Procession of the Sacred Object, Temporary Palace Wearing Ritual, and commencement announcement for the Upper Suwa Shrine Festival ** August 26: Evening Palace Ceremony ** August 27: Beach Descent Ceremony, Festival (unspecified), and Divine Blessing Festival ** August 28: Return Procession and conclusion announcement for the Upper Suwa Shrine Festival * Niiname-sai (新嘗祭): Late November (on Sunday) * Great Purification Ritual (大祓): December 31 == Local Information == ; Address: * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Fujisawa City|ja|藤沢市}}, Katase 2-chōme, 21-16 ; Transportation: * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kintetsu South Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}{{ill|Fujisawa Station|ja|藤沢駅}}; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|和書|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣}}|date=August 1932}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=535&cd=1206019&scd=&npg=0 {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社}}] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) *[http://katasesuwajinja.com/ Katase Suwa Shrine] (Official) *[http://www.suwayamyoujin.jp/ {{ill|Suwa Shrine Official Homepage|ja|諏訪神社 公式ホームページ}}) {{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すわしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Fujisawa City]] [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages, need dummy edit imported for attribution, if they are redirects the redirects need to be reviewed, adding jawiki link which must be reviewed to see if the page is already present here. Shimosha Torii |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} |location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}{{ill|Fujisawa City|ja|藤沢市}}{{ill|Katase (Fujisawa City)|ja|片瀬 (藤沢市)|lt=Katase|lt_ja=片瀬}} 2-chōme 21-16 (Shimosha) |位置 = Shimosha: {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|19|2.6|N|139|29|11.5|E|display=inline,title}}
Kamisha: {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|19|3.7|N|139|29|19.6|E|display=inline}} |deity = {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=建御名方命}}、{{ill|Yasakatotome-no-Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|lt=Yasakatotome-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=八坂刀売命}} |神体 = |type = Former village shrine (旧{{ill|Murasya|ja|村社}}) |創建 = In the 7th year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} ({{ill|723 AD|ja|723年}}) |本殿 = |別名 = Also known as Katase Suwa Shrine (片瀬諏訪神社) |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|August 27|ja|8月27日}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kanagawa|label=Suwa Shrine|float=center|width=210|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|35|19|2.6|N|139|29|11.5|E}}}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社, すわじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Fujisawa City|ja|藤沢市}}, Katase. It is also known as Katase Suwa Shrine (片瀬諏訪神社). The shrine is divided into two sections: the western Shimosha (lower shrine) and the eastern Kamisha (upper shrine). == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}} (Takeminakata-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Yasakatotome-no-Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命}} (Yasakatotome-no-Mikoto) == History == The shrine was founded in the 7th year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} ({{ill|723 AD|ja|723年}}). It enshrines the divine spirit of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, making it the oldest among those that received a portion of the spirit (御分霊) sent to other regions. The Shimosha was relocated from Miyabata in 812 AD (the 3rd year of {{ill|Kōnin|ja|弘仁}}), to a lakeside location with whale bones, while the Kamisha was relocated in 826 AD (the 3rd year of {{ill|Tenchō|ja|天長}}) from Suwa Gayatani to the mountainside of Nami-ai. In 1873 (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a village shrine. The shrine building of the Shimosha was rebuilt in 1941 (Showa 16), along with a new administrative office. Additionally, the one-bay shrine ({{ill|Ryū-zukuri|ja|流造}}) at the Kamisha was completed as part of the 60th Anniversary Celebration of the reign of the {{ill|Showa Emperor|ja|昭和天皇}}.[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=535&cd=1206019&scd=&npg=0 Suwa Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) In Reiwa 5 (2023), the shrine celebrated 1300 years since its establishment, with a 1300th Anniversary Celebration held on October 22. == Annual Festivals == * New Year's Festival (元旦祭): January 1 * Tanabata Festival: Mid-June to July 7 * Summer Grand Festival: ** August 23: Banner Raising, Procession of the Sacred Object, Temporary Palace Wearing Ritual, and commencement announcement for the Upper Suwa Shrine Festival ** August 26: Evening Palace Ceremony ** August 27: Beach Descent Ceremony, Festival (unspecified), and Divine Blessing Festival ** August 28: Return Procession and conclusion announcement for the Upper Suwa Shrine Festival * Niiname-sai (新嘗祭): Late November (on Sunday) * Great Purification Ritual (大祓): December 31 == Local Information == ; Address: * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Fujisawa City|ja|藤沢市}}, Katase 2-chōme, 21-16 ; Transportation: * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kintetsu South Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}{{ill|Fujisawa Station|ja|藤沢駅}}; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|和書|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣}}|date=August 1932}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=535&cd=1206019&scd=&npg=0 {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社}}] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) *[http://katasesuwajinja.com/ Katase Suwa Shrine] (Official) *[http://www.suwayamyoujin.jp/ {{ill|Suwa Shrine Official Homepage|ja|諏訪神社 公式ホームページ}}) {{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すわしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Fujisawa City]] [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages, need dummy edit imported for attribution, if they are redirects the redirects need to be reviewed, adding jawiki link which must be reviewed to see if the page is already present here. The shrine is divided into two sections: the western Shimosha (lower shrine) and the eastern Kamisha (upper shrine). == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}} (Takeminakata-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Yasakatotome-no-Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命}} (Yasakatotome-no-Mikoto) == History == The shrine was founded in the 7th year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} ({{ill|723 AD|ja|723年}}). The Shimosha was relocated from Miyabata in 812 AD (the 3rd year of {{ill|Kōnin|ja|弘仁}}), to a lakeside location with whale bones, while the Kamisha was relocated in 826 AD (the 3rd year of {{ill|Tenchō|ja|天長}}) from Suwa Gayatani to the mountainside of Nami-ai. In 1873 (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a village shrine. The shrine building of the Shimosha was rebuilt in 1941 (Showa 16), along with a new administrative office. Additionally, the one-bay shrine ({{ill|Ryū-zukuri|ja|流造}}) at the Kamisha was completed as part of the 60th Anniversary Celebration of the reign of the {{ill|Showa Emperor|ja|昭和天皇}}.[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=535&cd=1206019&scd=&npg=0 Suwa Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) In Reiwa 5 (2023), the shrine celebrated 1300 years since its establishment, with a 1300th Anniversary Celebration held on October 22. == Annual Festivals == * New Year's Festival (元旦祭): January 1 * Tanabata Festival: Mid-June to July 7 * Summer Grand Festival: ** August 23: Banner Raising, Procession of the Sacred Object, Temporary Palace Wearing Ritual, and commencement announcement for the Upper Suwa Shrine Festival ** August 26: Evening Palace Ceremony ** August 27: Beach Descent Ceremony, Festival (unspecified), and Divine Blessing Festival ** August 28: Return Procession and conclusion announcement for the Upper Suwa Shrine Festival * Niiname-sai (新嘗祭): Late November (on Sunday) * Great Purification Ritual (大祓): December 31 == Local Information == ; Address: * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Fujisawa City|ja|藤沢市}}, Katase 2-chōme, 21-16 ; Transportation: * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kintetsu South Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}{{ill|Fujisawa Station|ja|藤沢駅}}; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|和書|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣}}|date=August 1932}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=535&cd=1206019&scd=&npg=0 {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社}}] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) *[http://katasesuwajinja.com/ Katase Suwa Shrine] (Official) *[http://www.suwayamyoujin.jp/ {{ill|Suwa Shrine Official Homepage|ja|諏訪神社 公式ホームページ}}) {{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すわしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Fujisawa City]] [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages, need dummy edit imported for attribution, if they are redirects the redirects need to be reviewed, adding jawiki link which must be reviewed to see if the page is already present here. Later on the categories should be renamed too.]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (Fujisawa City Katase)]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages]][[Category:needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category: tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Suwa Shrine (Katase, Fujisawa City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Suwa Shrine (Katase, Fujisawa City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Suwa Shrine (Katase, Fujisawa City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Suwa Shrine (Katase, Fujisawa City)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (Fujisawa City Katase)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine (Katase, Fujisawa City)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine (Katase, Fujisawa City)}} [[Category:New]]

Kamisha: {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|19|3.7|N|139|29|19.6|E|display=inline}} |deity = {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=建御名方命}}、{{ill|Yasakatotome-no-Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|lt=Yasakatotome-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=八坂刀売命}} |神体 = |type = Former village shrine (旧{{ill|Murasya|ja|村社}}) |創建 = In the 7th year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} ({{ill|723 AD|ja|723rd year }}) |本殿 = |別名 = Also known as Katase Suwa Shrine (片瀬諏訪神社) |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|August 27|ja|8月27日}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kanagawa|label=Suwa Shrine|float=center|width=210|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|35|19|2.6|N|139|29|11.5|E}}}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社, すわじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Fujisawa City|ja|藤沢市}}, Katase. It is also known as Katase Suwa Shrine (片瀬諏訪神社). The shrine is divided into two sections: the western Shimosha (lower shrine) and the eastern Kamisha (upper shrine). == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}} (Takeminakata-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Yasakatotome-no-Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命}} (Yasakatotome-no-Mikoto) == History == The shrine was founded in the 7th year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} ({{ill|723 AD|ja|723rd year }}). It enshrines the divine spirit of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, making it the oldest among those that received a portion of the spirit (御分霊) sent to other regions. The Shimosha was relocated from Miyabata in 812 AD (the 3rd year of {{ill|Kōnin|ja|弘仁}}), to a lakeside location with whale bones, while the Kamisha was relocated in 826 AD (the 3rd year of {{ill|Tenchō|ja|天長}}) from Suwa Gayatani to the mountainside of Nami-ai. In 1873 (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a village shrine. The shrine building of the Shimosha was rebuilt in 1941 (Showa 16), along with a new administrative office. Additionally, the one-bay shrine ({{ill|Ryū-zukuri|ja|流造}}) at the Kamisha was completed as part of the 60th Anniversary Celebration of the reign of the {{ill|Showa Emperor|ja|昭和天皇}}.[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=535&cd=1206019&scd=&npg=0 Suwa Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) In Reiwa 5 (2023), the shrine celebrated 1300 years since its establishment, with a 1300th Anniversary Celebration held on October 22. == Annual Festivals == * New Year's Festival (元旦祭): January 1 * Tanabata Festival: Mid-June to July 7 * Summer Grand Festival: ** August 23: Banner Raising, Procession of the Sacred Object, Temporary Palace Wearing Ritual, and commencement announcement for the Upper Suwa Shrine Festival ** August 26: Evening Palace Ceremony ** August 27: Beach Descent Ceremony, Festival (unspecified), and Divine Blessing Festival ** August 28: Return Procession and conclusion announcement for the Upper Suwa Shrine Festival * Niiname-sai (新嘗祭): Late November (on Sunday) * Great Purification Ritual (大祓): December 31 == Local Information == ; Address: * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Fujisawa City|ja|藤沢市}}, Katase 2-chōme, 21-16 ; Transportation: * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kintetsu South Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}{{ill|Fujisawa Station|ja|藤沢駅}}; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|和書|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣}}|date=August 1932}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=535&cd=1206019&scd=&npg=0 {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社}}] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) *[http://katasesuwajinja.com/ Katase Suwa Shrine] (Official) *[http://www.suwayamyoujin.jp/ {{ill|Suwa Shrine Official Homepage|ja|諏訪神社 公式ホームページ}}) {{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すわしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Fujisawa City]] [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages, need dummy edit imported for attribution, if they are redirects the redirects need to be reviewed, adding jawiki link which must be reviewed to see if the page is already present here. Later on the categories should be renamed too.]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (Fujisawa City Katase)]] [[Category:needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category: tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Suwa Shrine (Katase, Fujisawa City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Suwa Shrine (Katase, Fujisawa City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Suwa Shrine (Katase, Fujisawa City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Suwa Shrine (Katase, Fujisawa City)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (Fujisawa City Katase)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine (Katase, Fujisawa City)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine (Katase, Fujisawa City)}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Suwa Shrine (Katase, Fujisawa City)]][[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]][[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]][[Category:qq]]

revision.comment: Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates.
revision.text:
{{Shrine |名称 = {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社}} |image = [[File:Suwa-jinja 001.jpg|thumb]]
Shimosha Torii |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} |location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}{{ill|Fujisawa City|ja|藤沢市}}{{ill|Katase (Fujisawa City)|ja|片瀬 (藤沢市)|lt=Katase|lt_ja=片瀬}} 2-chōme 21-16 (Shimosha) |位置 = Shimosha: {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|19|2.6|N|139|29|11.5|E|display=inline,title}}
Kamisha: {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|19|3.7|N|139|29|19.6|E|display=inline}} |deity = {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=建御名方命}}、{{ill|Yasakatotome-no-Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|lt=Yasakatotome-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=八坂刀売命}} |神体 = |type = Former village shrine (旧{{ill|Murasya|ja|村社}}) |創建 = In the 7th year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} ({{ill|723 AD|ja|723rd year }}) |本殿 = |別名 = Also known as Katase Suwa Shrine (片瀬諏訪神社) |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|August 27|ja|8月27日}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kanagawa|label=Suwa Shrine|float=center|width=210|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|35|19|2.6|N|139|29|11.5|E}}}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社, すわじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Fujisawa City|ja|藤沢市}}, Katase. It is also known as Katase Suwa Shrine (片瀬諏訪神社). The shrine is divided into two sections: the western Shimosha (lower shrine) and the eastern Kamisha (upper shrine). == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}} (Takeminakata-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Yasakatotome-no-Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命}} (Yasakatotome-no-Mikoto) == History == The shrine was founded in the 7th year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} ({{ill|723 AD|ja|723rd year }}). It enshrines the divine spirit of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, making it the oldest among those that received a portion of the spirit (御分霊) sent to other regions. The Shimosha was relocated from Miyabata in 812 AD (the 3rd year of {{ill|Kōnin|ja|弘仁}}), to a lakeside location with whale bones, while the Kamisha was relocated in 826 AD (the 3rd year of {{ill|Tenchō|ja|天長}}) from Suwa Gayatani to the mountainside of Nami-ai. In 1873 (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a village shrine. The shrine building of the Shimosha was rebuilt in 1941 (Showa 16), along with a new administrative office. Additionally, the one-bay shrine ({{ill|Ryū-zukuri|ja|流造}}) at the Kamisha was completed as part of the 60th Anniversary Celebration of the reign of the {{ill|Showa Emperor|ja|昭和天皇}}.[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=535&cd=1206019&scd=&npg=0 Suwa Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) In Reiwa 5 (2023), the shrine celebrated 1300 years since its establishment, with a 1300th Anniversary Celebration held on October 22. == Annual Festivals == * New Year's Festival (元旦祭): January 1 * Tanabata Festival: Mid-June to July 7 * Summer Grand Festival: ** August 23: Banner Raising, Procession of the Sacred Object, Temporary Palace Wearing Ritual, and commencement announcement for the Upper Suwa Shrine Festival ** August 26: Evening Palace Ceremony ** August 27: Beach Descent Ceremony, Festival (unspecified), and Divine Blessing Festival ** August 28: Return Procession and conclusion announcement for the Upper Suwa Shrine Festival * Niiname-sai (新嘗祭): Late November (on Sunday) * Great Purification Ritual (大祓): December 31 == Local Information == ; Address: * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Fujisawa City|ja|藤沢市}}, Katase 2-chōme, 21-16 ; Transportation: * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kintetsu South Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}{{ill|Fujisawa Station|ja|藤沢駅}}; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|和書|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣}}|date=August 1932}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=535&cd=1206019&scd=&npg=0 {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社}}] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) *[http://katasesuwajinja.com/ Katase Suwa Shrine] (Official) *[http://www.suwayamyoujin.jp/ {{ill|Suwa Shrine Official Homepage|ja|諏訪神社 公式ホームページ}}) {{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{draft categories| [[Category:藤沢市の神社]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shrines in Fujisawa City]] [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages, need dummy edit imported for attribution, if they are redirects the redirects need to be reviewed, adding jawiki link which must be reviewed to see if the page is already present here. Later on the categories should be renamed too.]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[ja:諏訪神社 (藤沢市片瀬)]] [[sn:Suwa Shrine (Katase, Fujisawa City)]] [[sn:諏訪神社 (藤沢市片瀬)]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (Fujisawa City Katase)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine (諏訪神社 (藤沢市片瀬))}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すわしんしや}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine (Katase, Fujisawa City)}}

revision.text:
{{Shrine |名称 = {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社|12=draft}} |image = [[File:Suwa-jinja 001.jpg|thumb]]
Shimosha Torii |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}} |location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|12=draft}}{{ill|Fujisawa City|ja|藤沢市|12=draft}}{{ill|Katase (Fujisawa City)|ja|片瀬 (藤沢市)|lt=Katase|lt_ja=片瀬|12=draft}} 2-chōme 21-16 (Shimosha) |位置 = Shimosha: {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|19|2.6|N|139|29|11.5|E|display=inline,title}}
Kamisha: {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|19|3.7|N|139|29|19.6|E|display=inline}} |deity = {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=建御名方命|12=draft}}、{{ill|Yasakatotome-no-Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|lt=Yasakatotome-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=八坂刀売命|12=draft}} |神体 = |type = Former village shrine (旧{{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|12=draft}}) |創建 = In the 7th year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老|12=draft}} ({{ill|723 AD|ja|723rd year|12=draft}}) |本殿 = |別名 = Also known as Katase Suwa Shrine (片瀬諏訪神社) |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|August 27|ja|8月27日|12=draft}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kanagawa|label=Suwa Shrine|float=center|width=210|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|35|19|2.6|N|139|29|11.5|E}}}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社, すわじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fujisawa City|ja|藤沢市|12=draft}}, Katase. It is also known as Katase Suwa Shrine (片瀬諏訪神社). The shrine is divided into two sections: the western Shimosha (lower shrine) and the eastern Kamisha (upper shrine). == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft}} (Takeminakata-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Yasakatotome-no-Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft}} (Yasakatotome-no-Mikoto) == History == The shrine was founded in the 7th year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老|12=draft}} ({{ill|723 AD|ja|723rd year|12=draft}}). It enshrines the divine spirit of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|12=draft}}, making it the oldest among those that received a portion of the spirit (御分霊) sent to other regions. The Shimosha was relocated from Miyabata in 812 AD (the 3rd year of {{ill|Kōnin|ja|弘仁|12=draft}}), to a lakeside location with whale bones, while the Kamisha was relocated in 826 AD (the 3rd year of {{ill|Tenchō|ja|天長|12=draft}}) from Suwa Gayatani to the mountainside of Nami-ai. In 1873 (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a village shrine. The shrine building of the Shimosha was rebuilt in 1941 (Showa 16), along with a new administrative office. Additionally, the one-bay shrine ({{ill|Ryū-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft}}) at the Kamisha was completed as part of the 60th Anniversary Celebration of the reign of the {{ill|Showa Emperor|ja|昭和天皇|12=draft}}.[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=535&cd=1206019&scd=&npg=0 Suwa Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) In Reiwa 5 (2023), the shrine celebrated 1300 years since its establishment, with a 1300th Anniversary Celebration held on October 22. == Annual Festivals == * New Year's Festival (元旦祭): January 1 * Tanabata Festival: Mid-June to July 7 * Summer Grand Festival: ** August 23: Banner Raising, Procession of the Sacred Object, Temporary Palace Wearing Ritual, and commencement announcement for the Upper Suwa Shrine Festival ** August 26: Evening Palace Ceremony ** August 27: Beach Descent Ceremony, Festival (unspecified), and Divine Blessing Festival ** August 28: Return Procession and conclusion announcement for the Upper Suwa Shrine Festival * Niiname-sai (新嘗祭): Late November (on Sunday) * Great Purification Ritual (大祓): December 31 == Local Information == ; Address: * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}} {{ill|Fujisawa City|ja|藤沢市|12=draft}}, Katase 2-chōme, 21-16 ; Transportation: * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kintetsu South Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|12=draft}}{{ill|Fujisawa Station|ja|藤沢駅|12=draft}}; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|和書|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣|12=draft}}|date=August 1932}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社|12=draft}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=535&cd=1206019&scd=&npg=0 {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社|12=draft}}] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) *[http://katasesuwajinja.com/ Katase Suwa Shrine] (Official) *[http://www.suwayamyoujin.jp/ {{ill|Suwa Shrine Official Homepage|ja|諏訪神社 公式ホームページ|12=draft}}) {{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{draft categories| [[Category:藤沢市の神社]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shrines in Fujisawa City]] [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages, need dummy edit imported for attribution, if they are redirects the redirects need to be reviewed, adding jawiki link which must be reviewed to see if the page is already present here. Later on the categories should be renamed too.]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:諏訪神社 (藤沢市片瀬)]] [[sn:Suwa Shrine (Katase, Fujisawa City)]] [[sn:諏訪神社 (藤沢市片瀬)]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (Fujisawa City Katase)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine (諏訪神社 (藤沢市片瀬))}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すわしんしや}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine (Katase, Fujisawa City)}}

approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|和書|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣|12=draft|12=draft}}|date=August 1932}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社|12=draft|12=draft}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft|12=draft}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=535&cd=1206019&scd=&npg=0 {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社|12=draft|12=draft}}] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) *[http://katasesuwajinja.com/ Katase Suwa Shrine] (Official) *[http://www.suwayamyoujin.jp/ {{ill|Suwa Shrine Official Homepage|ja|諏訪神社 公式ホームページ|12=draft|12=draft}}) {{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:藤沢市の神社]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shrines in Fujisawa City]] [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages, need dummy edit imported for attribution, if they are redirects the redirects need to be reviewed, adding jawiki link which must be reviewed to see if the page is already present here. Later on the categories should be renamed too.]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:諏訪神社 (藤沢市片瀬)]] [[sn:Suwa Shrine (Katase, Fujisawa City)]] [[sn:諏訪神社 (藤沢市片瀬)]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (Fujisawa City Katase)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine (諏訪神社 (藤沢市片瀬))}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すわしんしや}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine (Katase, Fujisawa City)}}

{{Shrine |名称 = {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |image = [[File:Suwa-jinja 001.jpg|thumb]]
Shimosha Torii |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Fujisawa City|ja|藤沢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Katase (Fujisawa City)|ja|片瀬 (藤沢市)|lt=Katase|lt_ja=片瀬|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2-chōme 21-16 (Shimosha) |位置 = Shimosha: {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|19|2.6|N|139|29|11.5|E|display=inline,title}}
Kamisha: {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|19|3.7|N|139|29|19.6|E|display=inline}} |deity = {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}、{{ill|Yasakatotome-no-Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|lt=Yasakatotome-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=八坂刀売命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |神体 = |type = Former village shrine (旧{{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) |創建 = In the 7th year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|723 AD|ja|723rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) |本殿 = |別名 = Also known as Katase Suwa Shrine (片瀬諏訪神社) |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|August 27|ja|8月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kanagawa|label=Suwa Shrine|float=center|width=210|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|35|19|2.6|N|139|29|11.5|E}}}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社, すわじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Fujisawa City|ja|藤沢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Katase. It is also known as Katase Suwa Shrine (片瀬諏訪神社). The shrine is divided into two sections: the western Shimosha (lower shrine) and the eastern Kamisha (upper shrine). == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Takeminakata-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Yasakatotome-no-Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yasakatotome-no-Mikoto) == History == The shrine was founded in the 7th year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|723 AD|ja|723rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). It enshrines the divine spirit of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, making it the oldest among those that received a portion of the spirit (御分霊) sent to other regions. The Shimosha was relocated from Miyabata in 812 AD (the 3rd year of {{ill|Kōnin|ja|弘仁|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), to a lakeside location with whale bones, while the Kamisha was relocated in 826 AD (the 3rd year of {{ill|Tenchō|ja|天長|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) from Suwa Gayatani to the mountainside of Nami-ai. In 1873 (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a village shrine. The shrine building of the Shimosha was rebuilt in 1941 (Showa 16), along with a new administrative office. Additionally, the one-bay shrine ({{ill|Ryū-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) at the Kamisha was completed as part of the 60th Anniversary Celebration of the reign of the {{ill|Showa Emperor|ja|昭和天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=535&cd=1206019&scd=&npg=0 Suwa Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) In Reiwa 5 (2023), the shrine celebrated 1300 years since its establishment, with a 1300th Anniversary Celebration held on October 22. == Annual Festivals == * New Year's Festival (元旦祭): January 1 * Tanabata Festival: Mid-June to July 7 * Summer Grand Festival: ** August 23: Banner Raising, Procession of the Sacred Object, Temporary Palace Wearing Ritual, and commencement announcement for the Upper Suwa Shrine Festival ** August 26: Evening Palace Ceremony ** August 27: Beach Descent Ceremony, Festival (unspecified), and Divine Blessing Festival ** August 28: Return Procession and conclusion announcement for the Upper Suwa Shrine Festival * Niiname-sai (新嘗祭): Late November (on Sunday) * Great Purification Ritual (大祓): December 31 == Local Information == ; Address: * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Fujisawa City|ja|藤沢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Katase 2-chōme, 21-16 ; Transportation: * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kintetsu South Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Fujisawa Station|ja|藤沢駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|和書|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|date=August 1932}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=535&cd=1206019&scd=&npg=0 {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) *[http://katasesuwajinja.com/ Katase Suwa Shrine] (Official) *[http://www.suwayamyoujin.jp/ {{ill|Suwa Shrine Official Homepage|ja|諏訪神社 公式ホームページ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) {{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:藤沢市の神社]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shrines in Fujisawa City]] [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages, need dummy edit imported for attribution, if they are redirects the redirects need to be reviewed, adding jawiki link which must be reviewed to see if the page is already present here. Later on the categories should be renamed too.]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:諏訪神社 (藤沢市片瀬)]] [[sn:Suwa Shrine (Katase, Fujisawa City)]] [[sn:諏訪神社 (藤沢市片瀬)]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (Fujisawa City Katase)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine (諏訪神社 (藤沢市片瀬))}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すわしんしや}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine (Katase, Fujisawa City)}}

revision.comment: 33 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:諏訪神社 (藤沢市片瀬) up to rev 103069700
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 諏訪神社 |画像=[[ファイル:Suwa-jinja 001.jpg|250px|藤沢市片瀬の諏訪神社]]
下社鳥居 |所在地 = [[神奈川県]][[藤沢市]][[片瀬 (藤沢市)|片瀬]]二丁目21番16号（下社） |位置 = 下社：{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|19|2.6|N|139|29|11.5|E|display=inline,title}}
上社：{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|19|3.7|N|139|29|19.6|E|display=inline}} |祭神 = [[建御名方神|建御名方命]]、[[八坂刀売神|八坂刀売命]] |神体 = |社格 = 旧[[村社]] |創建 = [[養老]]7年（[[723年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[8月27日]] |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map+|Japan Kanagawa|width=210|float=center|caption=|places= {{Location map~|Japan Kanagawa|lat_deg=35|lat_min=19|lat_sec=2.6|lon_deg=139|lon_min=29|lon_sec=11.5|position=left|label=下社|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{Location map~|Japan Kanagawa|lat_deg=35|lat_min=19|lat_sec=3.7|lon_deg=139|lon_min=29|lon_sec=19.6|position=right|label=上社|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}}} {{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|35.317569|N|139.487554|E}}|fullscreen-option=0|zoom=14|float=center|width=200|caption=|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|35|19|2.6|N|139|29|11.5|E}}|label1=下社|label-pos1=left|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|35|19|3.7|N|139|29|19.6|E}}|label2=上社|label-pos2=right|mark-size2=8}} }} '''諏訪神社'''（すわじんじゃ）は、[[神奈川県]][[藤沢市]][[片瀬 (藤沢市)|片瀬]]にある[[神社]]。地名を冠して'''片瀬諏訪神社'''（かたせすわじんじゃ）とも称される。西方の'''下社'''と、東方の'''上社'''に分かれる。 == 祭神 == * [[建御名方神|建御名方命]]（たけみなかたのみこと） * [[八坂刀売神|八坂刀売命]]（やさかとめのみこと） == 歴史 == 創建は[[養老]]7年（[[723年]]）。[[信濃国]]の[[諏訪大社]]を[[勧請]]しており、他郷へ御分霊した中で最古のものであるとされる。下社は[[弘仁]]3年（[[812年]]）に宮畑の地より鯨骨の湖畔に移転、上社は[[天長]]3年（[[826年]]）に諏訪ケ谷より浪合の山腹に移転している[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=535&cd=1206019&scd=&npg=0 諏訪神社]（神奈川県神社庁）。 [[1873年]]（[[明治]]6年）には、[[村社]]に列せられた。下社の[[神社建築|社殿]]は[[1941年]]（[[昭和]]16年）に改築されたもので、同時に[[神社#施設・設備|社務所]]も新築されている。また、上社における一間社[[流造]]の社殿は[[昭和天皇]]御在位六十年奉祝事業として完成したものである。 令和5年（2023年）に御鎮座1300年を迎え、10月22日に御鎮座1300年奉祝祭が執り行われた。 == 祭事・年間行事 == * [[元旦祭]]：[[1月1日]] * 七夕祭　6月中旬～7月7日 * 夏季大祭 ** [[8月23日]] *** 幟立て *** 出御祭 *** 仮宮着御祭 *** 上諏訪社例祭開始奉告祭 ** [[8月26日]] *** 宵宮 ** [[8月27日]] *** 浜降式 *** '''[[例祭]]''' *** [[神幸祭]] ** [[8月28日]] *** 還御祭 *** 上諏訪例祭終了奉告祭 * [[新嘗祭]]：[[11月]]下旬[[日曜日]] * [[大祓]]：[[12月31日]] == 神紋 == 当社の[[神紋]]は「[[梶の葉]]」で上社が「四本梶」、下社が「五本梶」となっている[https://zinzyasanpai.web.fc2.com/14/huzisawa/615.htm 諏訪神社 (神奈川県藤沢市片瀬)]（神社訪問記）。 == 所在地・交通 == 下社と上社間は距離にしておよそ200メートル離れており、徒歩3分程で移動できる。 ; 下社 所在地：神奈川県藤沢市片瀬二丁目21番16号（{{ウィキ座標|35|19|02.64|N|139|29|11.59|E|region:JP|alt=MAP|name=諏訪神社（片瀬）下社}}） {{osm box|n|2539816634|諏訪神社（片瀬）下社}} * 鉄道・モノレール ** [[江ノ島電鉄]]「[[湘南海岸公園駅]]」より徒歩約6分 ** [[湘南モノレール江の島線]]「[[目白山下駅]]」あるいは「[[湘南江の島駅]]」より徒歩約9分 ** [[小田急江ノ島線]]「[[片瀬江ノ島駅]]」より徒歩約12分 ; 上社 所在地：神奈川県藤沢市片瀬二丁目19番26号（{{ウィキ座標|35|19|03.63|N|139|29|19.91|E|region:JP|alt=MAP|name=諏訪神社（片瀬）上社}}） * 鉄道・モノレール ** 湘南モノレール江の島線「目白山下駅」より徒歩約8分 ** 江ノ島電鉄「湘南海岸公園駅」あるいは「[[江ノ島駅]]」より徒歩約9分 ** 小田急江ノ島線「片瀬江ノ島駅」より徒歩約15分 == その他 == 当社の[[管理]]は[[江島神社]]（藤沢市[[江の島]]）が兼務している。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''出典''' {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[諏訪神社]] - 諏訪大社を総本山として、全国各地に存在している。藤沢市内では大鋸にも[[諏訪神社 (藤沢市大鋸)|諏訪神社]]がある。 * [[神奈川県の神社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=535&cd=1206019&scd=&npg=0 諏訪神社]（神奈川県[[神社庁]]） * [http://katasesuwajinja.com/ 片瀬 諏訪神社]（公式） * {{Facebook||片瀬諏訪神社}} * {{Twitter||片瀬諏訪神社}} * {{Instagram||片瀬諏訪神社}} * [http://jinjajin.jp/modules/newdb/detail.php?id=7755 片瀬諏訪神社下社]（[[神社人]]） * [http://jinjajin.jp/modules/newdb/detail.php?id=7754 片瀬諏訪神社上社]（神社人） * [https://zinzyasanpai.web.fc2.com/14/huzisawa/615.htm 諏訪神社 (神奈川県藤沢市片瀬)]（神社訪問記） {{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:すわしんしや}} [[Category:藤沢市の神社]] [[Category:諏訪神社]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[諏訪神社 (藤沢市片瀬)]] to [[Suwa Shrine (Katase, Fujisawa City)]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 諏訪神社 |画像=[[ファイル:Suwa-jinja 001.jpg|250px|藤沢市片瀬の諏訪神社]]
下社鳥居 |所在地 = [[神奈川県]][[藤沢市]][[片瀬 (藤沢市)|片瀬]]二丁目21番16号（下社） |位置 = 下社：{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|19|2.6|N|139|29|11.5|E|display=inline,title}}
上社：{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|19|3.7|N|139|29|19.6|E|display=inline}} |祭神 = [[建御名方神|建御名方命]]、[[八坂刀売神|八坂刀売命]] |神体 = |社格 = 旧[[村社]] |創建 = [[養老]]7年（[[723年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[8月27日]] |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map+|Japan Kanagawa|width=210|float=center|caption=|places= {{Location map~|Japan Kanagawa|lat_deg=35|lat_min=19|lat_sec=2.6|lon_deg=139|lon_min=29|lon_sec=11.5|position=left|label=下社|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{Location map~|Japan Kanagawa|lat_deg=35|lat_min=19|lat_sec=3.7|lon_deg=139|lon_min=29|lon_sec=19.6|position=right|label=上社|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}}} {{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|35.317569|N|139.487554|E}}|fullscreen-option=0|zoom=14|float=center|width=200|caption=|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|35|19|2.6|N|139|29|11.5|E}}|label1=下社|label-pos1=left|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|35|19|3.7|N|139|29|19.6|E}}|label2=上社|label-pos2=right|mark-size2=8}} }} '''諏訪神社'''（すわじんじゃ）は、[[神奈川県]][[藤沢市]][[片瀬 (藤沢市)|片瀬]]にある[[神社]]。地名を冠して'''片瀬諏訪神社'''（かたせすわじんじゃ）とも称される。西方の'''下社'''と、東方の'''上社'''に分かれる。 == 祭神 == * [[建御名方神|建御名方命]]（たけみなかたのみこと） * [[八坂刀売神|八坂刀売命]]（やさかとめのみこと） == 歴史 == 創建は[[養老]]7年（[[723年]]）。[[信濃国]]の[[諏訪大社]]を[[勧請]]しており、他郷へ御分霊した中で最古のものであるとされる。下社は[[弘仁]]3年（[[812年]]）に宮畑の地より鯨骨の湖畔に移転、上社は[[天長]]3年（[[826年]]）に諏訪ケ谷より浪合の山腹に移転している[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=535&cd=1206019&scd=&npg=0 諏訪神社]（神奈川県神社庁）。 [[1873年]]（[[明治]]6年）には、[[村社]]に列せられた。下社の[[神社建築|社殿]]は[[1941年]]（[[昭和]]16年）に改築されたもので、同時に[[神社#施設・設備|社務所]]も新築されている。また、上社における一間社[[流造]]の社殿は[[昭和天皇]]御在位六十年奉祝事業として完成したものである。 令和5年（2023年）に御鎮座1300年を迎え、10月22日に御鎮座1300年奉祝祭が執り行われた。 == 祭事・年間行事 == * [[元旦祭]]：[[1月1日]] * 七夕祭　6月中旬～7月7日 * 夏季大祭 ** [[8月23日]] *** 幟立て *** 出御祭 *** 仮宮着御祭 *** 上諏訪社例祭開始奉告祭 ** [[8月26日]] *** 宵宮 ** [[8月27日]] *** 浜降式 *** '''[[例祭]]''' *** [[神幸祭]] ** [[8月28日]] *** 還御祭 *** 上諏訪例祭終了奉告祭 * [[新嘗祭]]：[[11月]]下旬[[日曜日]] * [[大祓]]：[[12月31日]] == 神紋 == 当社の[[神紋]]は「[[梶の葉]]」で上社が「四本梶」、下社が「五本梶」となっている[https://zinzyasanpai.web.fc2.com/14/huzisawa/615.htm 諏訪神社 (神奈川県藤沢市片瀬)]（神社訪問記）。 == 所在地・交通 == 下社と上社間は距離にしておよそ200メートル離れており、徒歩3分程で移動できる。 ; 下社 所在地：神奈川県藤沢市片瀬二丁目21番16号（{{ウィキ座標|35|19|02.64|N|139|29|11.59|E|region:JP|alt=MAP|name=諏訪神社（片瀬）下社}}） {{osm box|n|2539816634|諏訪神社（片瀬）下社}} * 鉄道・モノレール ** [[江ノ島電鉄]]「[[湘南海岸公園駅]]」より徒歩約6分 ** [[湘南モノレール江の島線]]「[[目白山下駅]]」あるいは「[[湘南江の島駅]]」より徒歩約9分 ** [[小田急江ノ島線]]「[[片瀬江ノ島駅]]」より徒歩約12分 ; 上社 所在地：神奈川県藤沢市片瀬二丁目19番26号（{{ウィキ座標|35|19|03.63|N|139|29|19.91|E|region:JP|alt=MAP|name=諏訪神社（片瀬）上社}}） * 鉄道・モノレール ** 湘南モノレール江の島線「目白山下駅」より徒歩約8分 ** 江ノ島電鉄「湘南海岸公園駅」あるいは「[[江ノ島駅]]」より徒歩約9分 ** 小田急江ノ島線「片瀬江ノ島駅」より徒歩約15分 == その他 == 当社の[[管理]]は[[江島神社]]（藤沢市[[江の島]]）が兼務している。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''出典''' {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[諏訪神社]] - 諏訪大社を総本山として、全国各地に存在している。藤沢市内では大鋸にも[[諏訪神社 (藤沢市大鋸)|諏訪神社]]がある。 * [[神奈川県の神社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=535&cd=1206019&scd=&npg=0 諏訪神社]（神奈川県[[神社庁]]） * [http://katasesuwajinja.com/ 片瀬 諏訪神社]（公式） * {{Facebook||片瀬諏訪神社}} * {{Twitter||片瀬諏訪神社}} * {{Instagram||片瀬諏訪神社}} * [http://jinjajin.jp/modules/newdb/detail.php?id=7755 片瀬諏訪神社下社]（[[神社人]]） * [http://jinjajin.jp/modules/newdb/detail.php?id=7754 片瀬諏訪神社上社]（神社人） * [https://zinzyasanpai.web.fc2.com/14/huzisawa/615.htm 諏訪神社 (神奈川県藤沢市片瀬)]（神社訪問記） {{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:すわしんしや}} revision.text:
{{Shrine |名称 = {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |image = [[File:Suwa-jinja 001.jpg|thumb]]
Shimosha Torii |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Fujisawa City|ja|藤沢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Katase (Fujisawa City)|ja|片瀬 (藤沢市)|lt=Katase|lt_ja=片瀬|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2-chōme 21-16 (Shimosha) |位置 = Shimosha: {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|19|2.6|N|139|29|11.5|E|display=inline,title}}
Kamisha: {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|19|3.7|N|139|29|19.6|E|display=inline}} |deity = {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}、{{ill|Yasakatotome-no-Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|lt=Yasakatotome-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=八坂刀売命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |神体 = |type = Former village shrine (旧{{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) |創建 = In the 7th year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|723 AD|ja|723rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) |本殿 = |別名 = Also known as Katase Suwa Shrine (片瀬諏訪神社) |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|August 27|ja|8月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kanagawa|label=Suwa Shrine|float=center|width=210|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|35|19|2.6|N|139|29|11.5|E}}}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社, すわじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Fujisawa City|ja|藤沢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Katase. It is also known as Katase Suwa Shrine (片瀬諏訪神社). The shrine is divided into two sections: the western Shimosha (lower shrine) and the eastern Kamisha (upper shrine). == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Takeminakata-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Yasakatotome-no-Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yasakatotome-no-Mikoto) == History == The shrine was founded in the 7th year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|723 AD|ja|723rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). It enshrines the divine spirit of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, making it the oldest among those that received a portion of the spirit (御分霊) sent to other regions. The Shimosha was relocated from Miyabata in 812 AD (the 3rd year of {{ill|Kōnin|ja|弘仁|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), to a lakeside location with whale bones, while the Kamisha was relocated in 826 AD (the 3rd year of {{ill|Tenchō|ja|天長|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) from Suwa Gayatani to the mountainside of Nami-ai. In 1873 (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a village shrine. The shrine building of the Shimosha was rebuilt in 1941 (Showa 16), along with a new administrative office. Additionally, the one-bay shrine ({{ill|Ryū-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) at the Kamisha was completed as part of the 60th Anniversary Celebration of the reign of the {{ill|Showa Emperor|ja|昭和天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=535&cd=1206019&scd=&npg=0 Suwa Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) In Reiwa 5 (2023), the shrine celebrated 1300 years since its establishment, with a 1300th Anniversary Celebration held on October 22. == Annual Festivals == * New Year's Festival (元旦祭): January 1 * Tanabata Festival: Mid-June to July 7 * Summer Grand Festival: ** August 23: Banner Raising, Procession of the Sacred Object, Temporary Palace Wearing Ritual, and commencement announcement for the Upper Suwa Shrine Festival ** August 26: Evening Palace Ceremony ** August 27: Beach Descent Ceremony, Festival (unspecified), and Divine Blessing Festival ** August 28: Return Procession and conclusion announcement for the Upper Suwa Shrine Festival * Niiname-sai (新嘗祭): Late November (on Sunday) * Great Purification Ritual (大祓): December 31 == Local Information == ; Address: * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Fujisawa City|ja|藤沢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Katase 2-chōme, 21-16 ; Transportation: * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kintetsu South Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Fujisawa Station|ja|藤沢駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|和書|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|date=August 1932}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=535&cd=1206019&scd=&npg=0 {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) *[http://katasesuwajinja.com/ Katase Suwa Shrine] (Official) *[http://www.suwayamyoujin.jp/ {{ill|Suwa Shrine Official Homepage|ja|諏訪神社 公式ホームページ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) {{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine (諏訪神社 (藤沢市片瀬))}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すわしんしや}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine (Katase, Fujisawa City)}} {{translated page|ja|諏訪神社 (藤沢市片瀬)|version=103069700|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:藤沢市の神社]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shrines in Fujisawa City]] [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages, need dummy edit imported for attribution, if they are redirects the redirects need to be reviewed, adding jawiki link which must be reviewed to see if the page is already present here. Later on the categories should be renamed too.]] }} [[ja:諏訪神社 (藤沢市片瀬)]] [[sn:Suwa Shrine (Katase, Fujisawa City)]] [[sn:諏訪神社 (藤沢市片瀬)]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (Fujisawa City Katase)]]
