[[ja:諏訪神社 (藤沢市片瀬)]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (藤沢市片瀬)]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (藤沢市片瀬)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称 = {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine|qq=}} |image = File:Suwa-jinja 001.jpg |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}} |location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|Fujisawa, Kanagawa|en|Fujisawa, Kanagawa|WD=Q386661|lt=Fujisawa City}}{{ill|Katase (Fujisawa City)|ja|片瀬 (藤沢市)|lt=Katase|lt_ja=片瀬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katase (Fujisawa City)|1=Katase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11569905}} 2-chōme 21-16 (Shimosha) |位置 = Shimosha: {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|19|2.6|N|139|29|11.5|E|display=inline,title}}
Kamisha: {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|19|3.7|N|139|29|19.6|E|display=inline}} |deity = {{ill|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391}} |神体 = |type = Former Village shrine (旧{{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}) |創建 = In the 7th year of {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō}} ({{ill|723|en|723|WD=Q23545|lt=723 AD}}) |本殿 = |別名 = Also known as Katase Suwa Shrine (片瀬諏訪神社) |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|August 27|en|August 27|WD=Q2822|lt=August 27}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kanagawa|label=Suwa Shrine|float=center|width=210|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|35|19|2.6|N|139|29|11.5|E}}}} |prefecture = {{ill|Fujisawa|WD=Q386661}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.3174|N|139.48655278|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社, すわじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Fujisawa, Kanagawa|en|Fujisawa, Kanagawa|WD=Q386661|lt=Fujisawa City}}, Katase. It is also known as Katase Suwa Shrine (片瀬諏訪神社). The shrine is divided into two sections: the western Shimosha (lower shrine) and the eastern Kamisha (upper shrine). == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ|1=Takeminakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9150391}} (Takeminakata-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Yasakatotome-no-Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|1=Yasakatome|2=ja|3=八坂刀売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakatotome-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂刀売神}} (Yasakatotome-no-Mikoto) == History == The shrine was founded in the 7th year of {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō}} ({{ill|723|en|723|WD=Q23545|lt=723 AD}}). It enshrines the divine spirit of {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa Taisha}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province}}, making it the oldest among those that received a portion of the spirit (御分霊) sent to other regions. The Shimosha was relocated from Miyabata in 812 AD (the 3rd year of {{ill|Kōnin (era)|en|Kōnin (era)|WD=Q1373070|lt=Kōnin}}), to a lakeside location with whale bones, while the Kamisha was relocated in 826 AD (the 3rd year of {{ill|Tenchō|en|Tenchō|WD=Q1373138|lt=Tenchō}}) from Suwa Gayatani to the mountainside of Nami-ai. In 1873 (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a village shrine. The shrine building of the Shimosha was rebuilt in 1941 (Showa 16), along with a new administrative office. Additionally, the one-bay shrine ({{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Ryū-zukuri}}) at the Kamisha was completed as part of the 60th Anniversary Celebration of the reign of the {{ill|Hirohito|en|Hirohito|WD=Q34479|lt=Showa Emperor}}.[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=535&cd=1206019&scd=&npg=0 Suwa Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) In Reiwa 5 (2023), the shrine celebrated 1300 years since its establishment, with a 1300th Anniversary Celebration held on October 22. == Annual Festivals == * New Year's Festival (元旦祭): January 1 * Tanabata Festival: Mid-June to July 7 * Summer Grand Festival: ** August 23: Banner Raising, Procession of the Sacred Object, Temporary Palace Wearing Ritual, and commencement announcement for the Upper Suwa Shrine Festival ** August 26: Evening Palace Ceremony ** August 27: Beach Descent Ceremony, Festival (unspecified), and Divine Blessing Festival ** August 28: Return Procession and conclusion announcement for the Upper Suwa Shrine Festival * Niiname-sai (新嘗祭): Late November (on Sunday) * Great Purification Ritual (大祓): December 31 == Local Information == ; Address: * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}} {{ill|Fujisawa, Kanagawa|en|Fujisawa, Kanagawa|WD=Q386661|lt=Fujisawa City}}, Katase 2-chōme, 21-16 ; Transportation: * Nearest Station: {{ill|Minami Osaka Line|en|Minami Osaka Line|WD=Q901342|lt=Kintetsu South Osaka Line}}{{ill|Fujisawa Station|en|Fujisawa Station|WD=Q1133110|lt=Fujisawa Station}}; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|和書|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuzan-kaku|1=Yuzankaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659206}}|date=August 1932}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|1=Takaya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669282}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=535&cd=1206019&scd=&npg=0 {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine|qq=}}] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) *[http://katasesuwajinja.com/ Katase Suwa Shrine] (Official) *[http://www.suwayamyoujin.jp/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Suwa Shrine Official Homepage|ja|諏訪神社 公式ホームページ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine Official Homepage|qq=}}) {{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|諏訪神社 (藤沢市片瀬)|version=103069700|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:諏訪神社 (藤沢市片瀬)]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (Fujisawa City Katase)]] {{wikidata link|Q17994656}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages, need dummy edit imported for attribution, if they are redirects the redirects need to be reviewed, adding jawiki link which must be reviewed to see if the page is already present here. Later on the categories should be renamed too.]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fujisawa]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed parentheses]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:pages]]

revision.text:
[[ja:諏訪神社 (藤沢市片瀬)]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (藤沢市片瀬)]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (藤沢市片瀬)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称 = {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine|qq=}} |image = File:Suwa-jinja 001.jpg |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}} |location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|Fujisawa, Kanagawa|en|Fujisawa, Kanagawa|WD=Q386661|lt=Fujisawa City}}{{ill|Katase (Fujisawa City)|ja|片瀬 (藤沢市)|lt=Katase|lt_ja=片瀬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katase (Fujisawa City)|1=Katase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11569905}} 2-chōme 21-16 (Shimosha) |位置 = Shimosha: {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|19|2.6|N|139|29|11.5|E|display=inline,title}}
Kamisha: {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|19|3.7|N|139|29|19.6|E|display=inline}} |deity = {{ill|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391}} |神体 = |type = Former Village shrine (旧{{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}) |創建 = In the 7th year of {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō}} ({{ill|723|en|723|WD=Q23545|lt=723 AD}}) |本殿 = |別名 = Also known as Katase Suwa Shrine (片瀬諏訪神社) |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|August 27|en|August 27|WD=Q2822|lt=August 27}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kanagawa|label=Suwa Shrine|float=center|width=210|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|35|19|2.6|N|139|29|11.5|E}}}} |prefecture = {{ill|Fujisawa|WD=Q386661}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.3174|N|139.48655278|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社, すわじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Fujisawa, Kanagawa|en|Fujisawa, Kanagawa|WD=Q386661|lt=Fujisawa City}}, Katase. It is also known as Katase Suwa Shrine (片瀬諏訪神社). The shrine is divided into two sections: the western Shimosha (lower shrine) and the eastern Kamisha (upper shrine). == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ|1=Takeminakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9150391}} (Takeminakata-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Yasakatotome-no-Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|1=Yasakatome|2=ja|3=八坂刀売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakatotome-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂刀売神}} (Yasakatotome-no-Mikoto) == History == The shrine was founded in the 7th year of {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō}} ({{ill|723|en|723|WD=Q23545|lt=723 AD}}). It enshrines the divine spirit of {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa Taisha}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province}}, making it the oldest among those that received a portion of the spirit (御分霊) sent to other regions. The Shimosha was relocated from Miyabata in 812 AD (the 3rd year of {{ill|Kōnin (era)|en|Kōnin (era)|WD=Q1373070|lt=Kōnin}}), to a lakeside location with whale bones, while the Kamisha was relocated in 826 AD (the 3rd year of {{ill|Tenchō|en|Tenchō|WD=Q1373138|lt=Tenchō}}) from Suwa Gayatani to the mountainside of Nami-ai. In 1873 (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a village shrine. The shrine building of the Shimosha was rebuilt in 1941 (Showa 16), along with a new administrative office. Additionally, the one-bay shrine ({{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Ryū-zukuri}}) at the Kamisha was completed as part of the 60th Anniversary Celebration of the reign of the {{ill|Hirohito|en|Hirohito|WD=Q34479|lt=Showa Emperor}}.[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=535&cd=1206019&scd=&npg=0 Suwa Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) In Reiwa 5 (2023), the shrine celebrated 1300 years since its establishment, with a 1300th Anniversary Celebration held on October 22. == Annual Festivals == * New Year's Festival (元旦祭): January 1 * Tanabata Festival: Mid-June to July 7 * Summer Grand Festival: ** August 23: Banner Raising, Procession of the Sacred Object, Temporary Palace Wearing Ritual, and commencement announcement for the Upper Suwa Shrine Festival ** August 26: Evening Palace Ceremony ** August 27: Beach Descent Ceremony, Festival (unspecified), and Divine Blessing Festival ** August 28: Return Procession and conclusion announcement for the Upper Suwa Shrine Festival * Niiname-sai (新嘗祭): Late November (on Sunday) * Great Purification Ritual (大祓): December 31 == Local Information == ; Address: * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}} {{ill|Fujisawa, Kanagawa|en|Fujisawa, Kanagawa|WD=Q386661|lt=Fujisawa City}}, Katase 2-chōme, 21-16 ; Transportation: * Nearest Station: {{ill|Minami Osaka Line|en|Minami Osaka Line|WD=Q901342|lt=Kintetsu South Osaka Line}}{{ill|Fujisawa Station|en|Fujisawa Station|WD=Q1133110|lt=Fujisawa Station}}; approximately an 8-minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|和書|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuzan-kaku|1=Yuzankaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659206}}|date=August 1932}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa [[ja:諏訪神社 (藤沢市片瀬)]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (藤沢市片瀬)]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (藤沢市片瀬)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称 = {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine|qq=}} |image = File:Suwa-jinja 001.jpg |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} |location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|Fujisawa, Kanagawa|en|Fujisawa, Kanagawa|WD=Q386661|lt=Fujisawa City|1=Fujisawa, Kanagawa}}{{ill|Katase (Fujisawa City)|ja|片瀬 (藤沢市)|lt=Katase|lt_ja=片瀬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katase (Fujisawa City)|1=Katase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11569905}} 2-chōme 21-16 (Shimosha) |位置 = Shimosha: {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|19|2.6|N|139|29|11.5|E|display=inline,title}}
Kamisha: {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|19|3.7|N|139|29|19.6|E|display=inline}} |deity = {{ill|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|1=Takeminakata}} |神体 = |type = Former Village shrine (旧{{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}) |創建 = In the 7th year of {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō|1=Yōrō}} ({{ill|723|en|723|WD=Q23545|lt=723 AD|1=723}}) |本殿 = |別名 = Also known as Katase Suwa Shrine (片瀬諏訪神社) |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|August 27|en|August 27|WD=Q2822|lt=August 27|1=August 27}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kanagawa|label=Suwa Shrine|float=center|width=210|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|35|19|2.6|N|139|29|11.5|E}}}} |prefecture = {{ill|Fujisawa|WD=Q386661|1=Fujisawa, Kanagawa}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.3174|N|139.48655278|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社, すわじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Fujisawa, Kanagawa|en|Fujisawa, Kanagawa|WD=Q386661|lt=Fujisawa City|1=Fujisawa, Kanagawa}}, Katase. It is also known as Katase Suwa Shrine (片瀬諏訪神社). The shrine is divided into two sections: the western Shimosha (lower shrine) and the eastern Kamisha (upper shrine). == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ|1=Takeminakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9150391}} (Takeminakata-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Yasakatotome-no-Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|1=Yasakatome|2=ja|3=八坂刀売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakatotome-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂刀売神}} (Yasakatotome-no-Mikoto) == History == The shrine was founded in the 7th year of {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō|1=Yōrō}} ({{ill|723|en|723|WD=Q23545|lt=723 AD|1=723}}). It enshrines the divine spirit of {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa Taisha|1=Suwa-taisha}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province|1=Shinano Province}}, making it the oldest among those that received a portion of the spirit (御分霊) sent to other regions. The Shimosha was relocated from Miyabata in 812 AD (the 3rd year of {{ill|Kōnin (era)|en|Kōnin (era)|WD=Q1373070|lt=Kōnin|1=Kōnin (era)}}), to a lakeside location with whale bones, while the Kamisha was relocated in 826 AD (the 3rd year of {{ill|Tenchō|en|Tenchō|WD=Q1373138|lt=Tenchō|1=Tenchō}}) from Suwa Gayatani to the mountainside of Nami-ai. In 1873 (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a village shrine. The shrine building of the Shimosha was rebuilt in 1941 (Showa 16), along with a new administrative office. Additionally, the one-bay shrine ({{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Ryū-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}}) at the Kamisha was completed as part of the 60th Anniversary Celebration of the reign of the {{ill|Hirohito|en|Hirohito|WD=Q34479|lt=Showa Emperor|1=Hirohito}}.[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=535&cd=1206019&scd=&npg=0 Suwa Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) In Reiwa 5 (2023), the shrine celebrated 1300 years since its establishment, with a 1300th Anniversary Celebration held on October 22. == Annual Festivals == * New Year's Festival (元旦祭): January 1 * Tanabata Festival: Mid-June to July 7 * Summer Grand Festival: ** August 23: Banner Raising, Procession of the Sacred Object, Temporary Palace Wearing Ritual, and commencement announcement for the Upper Suwa Shrine Festival ** August 26: Evening Palace Ceremony ** August 27: Beach Descent Ceremony, Festival (unspecified), and Divine Blessing Festival ** August 28: Return Procession and conclusion announcement for the Upper Suwa Shrine Festival * Niiname-sai (新嘗祭): Late November (on Sunday) * Great Purification Ritual (大祓): December 31 == Local Information == ; Address: * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}} {{ill|Fujisawa, Kanagawa|en|Fujisawa, Kanagawa|WD=Q386661|lt=Fujisawa City|1=Fujisawa, Kanagawa}}, Katase 2-chōme, 21-16 ; Transportation: * Nearest Station: {{ill|Minami Osaka Line|en|Minami Osaka Line|WD=Q901342|lt=Kintetsu South Osaka Line|1=Minami Osaka Line}}{{ill|Fujisawa Station|en|Fujisawa Station|WD=Q1133110|lt=Fujisawa Station|1=Fujisawa Station}}; approximately an 8-minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|和書|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuzan-kaku|1=Yuzankaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659206}}|date=August 1932}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|1=Takaya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669282}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=535&cd=1206019&scd=&npg=0 {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine|qq=}}] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) *[http://katasesuwajinja.com/ Katase Suwa Shrine] (Official) *[http://www.suwayamyoujin.jp/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Suwa Shrine Official Homepage|ja|諏訪神社 公式ホームページ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine Official Homepage|qq=}}) {{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|諏訪神社 (藤沢市片瀬)|version=103069700|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:諏訪神社 (藤沢市片瀬)]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (Fujisawa City Katase)]] {{wikidata link|Q17994656}} [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages, need dummy edit imported for attribution, if they are redirects the redirects need to be reviewed, adding jawiki link which must be reviewed to see if the page is already present here. Later on the categories should be renamed too.]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fujisawa]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed parentheses]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:pages]]

Additionally, the one-bay shrine ({{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Ryū-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}}) at the Kamisha was completed as part of the 60th Anniversary Celebration of the reign of the {{ill|Hirohito|en|Hirohito|WD=Q34479|lt=Showa Emperor|1=Hirohito}}.[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=535&cd=1206019&scd=&npg=0 Suwa Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) In Reiwa 5 (2023), the shrine celebrated 1300 years since its establishment, with a 1300th Anniversary Celebration held on October 22. == Annual Festivals == * New Year's Festival (元旦祭): January 1 * Tanabata Festival: Mid-June to July 7 * Summer Grand Festival: ** August 23: Banner Raising, Procession of the Sacred Object, Temporary Palace Wearing Ritual, and commencement announcement for the Upper Suwa Shrine Festival ** August 26: Evening Palace Ceremony ** August 27: Beach Descent Ceremony, Festival (unspecified), and Divine Blessing Festival ** August 28: Return Procession and conclusion announcement for the Upper Suwa Shrine Festival * Niiname-sai (新嘗祭): Late November (on Sunday) * Great Purification Ritual (大祓): December 31 == Local Information == ; Address: * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}} {{ill|Fujisawa, Kanagawa|en|Fujisawa, Kanagawa|WD=Q386661|lt=Fujisawa City|1=Fujisawa, Kanagawa}}, Katase 2-chōme, 21-16 ; Transportation: * Nearest Station: {{ill|Minami Osaka Line|en|Minami Osaka Line|WD=Q901342|lt=Kintetsu South Osaka Line|1=Minami Osaka Line}}{{ill|Fujisawa Station|en|Fujisawa Station|WD=Q1133110|lt=Fujisawa Station|1=Fujisawa Station}}; approximately an 8-minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|和書|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, wikidata|WD=Q11669282}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} [[ja:諏訪神社 (藤沢市片瀬)]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (藤沢市片瀬)]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (藤沢市片瀬)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称 = {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine|qq=}} |image = File:Suwa-jinja 001.jpg |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} |location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|Fujisawa, Kanagawa|en|Fujisawa, Kanagawa|WD=Q386661|lt=Fujisawa City|1=Fujisawa, Kanagawa}}{{ill|Katase (Fujisawa City)|ja|片瀬 (藤沢市)|lt=Katase|lt_ja=片瀬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katase (Fujisawa City)|1=Katase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11569905}} 2-chōme 21-16 (Shimosha) |位置 = Shimosha: {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|19|2.6|N|139|29|11.5|E|display=inline,title}}
Kamisha: {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|19|3.7|N|139|29|19.6|E|display=inline}} |deity = {{ill|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|1=Takeminakata}} |神体 = |type = Former Village shrine (旧{{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}) |創建 = In the 7th year of {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō|1=Yōrō}} ({{ill|723|en|723|WD=Q23545|lt=723 AD|1=723}}) |本殿 = |別名 = Also known as Katase Suwa Shrine (片瀬諏訪神社) |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|August 27|en|August 27|WD=Q2822|lt=August 27|1=August 27}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kanagawa|label=Suwa Shrine|float=center|width=210|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|35|19|2.6|N|139|29|11.5|E}}}} |prefecture = {{ill|Fujisawa|WD=Q386661|1=Fujisawa, Kanagawa}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.3174|N|139.48655278|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社, すわじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Fujisawa, Kanagawa|en|Fujisawa, Kanagawa|WD=Q386661|lt=Fujisawa City|1=Fujisawa, Kanagawa}}, Katase. It is also known as Katase Suwa Shrine (片瀬諏訪神社). The shrine is divided into two sections: the western Shimosha (lower shrine) and the eastern Kamisha (upper shrine). == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ|1=Takeminakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9150391}} (Takeminakata-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Yasakatotome-no-Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|1=Yasakatome|2=ja|3=八坂刀売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakatotome-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂刀売神}} (Yasakatotome-no-Mikoto) == History == The shrine was founded in the 7th year of {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō|1=Yōrō}} ({{ill|723|en|723|WD=Q23545|lt=723 AD|1=723}}). It enshrines the divine spirit of {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa Taisha|1=Suwa-taisha}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province|1=Shinano Province}}, making it the oldest among those that received a portion of the spirit (御分霊) sent to other regions. The Shimosha was relocated from Miyabata in 812 AD (the 3rd year of {{ill|Kōnin (era)|en|Kōnin (era)|WD=Q1373070|lt=Kōnin|1=Kōnin (era)}}), to a lakeside location with whale bones, while the Kamisha was relocated in 826 AD (the 3rd year of {{ill|Tenchō|en|Tenchō|WD=Q1373138|lt=Tenchō|1=Tenchō}}) from Suwa Gayatani to the mountainside of Nami-ai. In 1873 (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a village shrine. The shrine building of the Shimosha was rebuilt in 1941 (Showa 16), along with a new administrative office. Additionally, the one-bay shrine ({{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Ryū-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}}) at the Kamisha was completed as part of the 60th Anniversary Celebration of the reign of the {{ill|Hirohito|en|Hirohito|WD=Q34479|lt=Showa Emperor|1=Hirohito}}.[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=535&cd=1206019&scd=&npg=0 Suwa Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Agency) In Reiwa 5 (2023), the shrine celebrated 1300 years since its establishment, with a 1300th Anniversary Celebration held on October 22. == Annual Festivals == * New Year's Festival (元旦祭): January 1 * Tanabata Festival: Mid-June to July 7 * Summer Grand Festival: ** August 23: Banner Raising, Procession of the Sacred Object, Temporary Palace Wearing Ritual, and commencement announcement for the Upper Suwa Shrine Festival ** August 26: Evening Palace Ceremony ** August 27: Beach Descent Ceremony, Festival (unspecified), and Divine Blessing Festival ** August 28: Return Procession and conclusion announcement for the Upper Suwa Shrine Festival * Niiname-sai (新嘗祭): Late November (on Sunday) * Great Purification Ritual (大祓): December 31 == Local Information == ; Address: * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}} {{ill|Fujisawa, Kanagawa|en|Fujisawa, Kanagawa|WD=Q386661|lt=Fujisawa City|1=Fujisawa, Kanagawa}}, Katase 2-chōme, 21-16 ; Transportation: * Nearest Station: {{ill|Minami Osaka Line|en|Minami Osaka Line|WD=Q901342|lt=Kintetsu South Osaka Line|1=Minami Osaka Line}}{{ill|Fujisawa Station|en|Fujisawa Station|WD=Q1133110|lt=Fujisawa Station|1=Fujisawa Station}}; approximately an 8-minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite
